title: Mexican Tagine Flavors Potato Tacos
description: A fusion of Mexican flavors and traditional Moroccan tagine, these potato tacos are a unique and delicious twist on a classic dish.
instructions: Peel the potatoes and cut them into 1/2-inch cubes. Place them in a bowl and drizzle with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, tossing to coat.
Sprinkle the tagine over the potatoes and toss to combine.
Season with salt to taste. Spread the potatoes in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
While the potatoes are roasting, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 10-12 minutes.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Remove the potatoes from the oven and stir in the lime juice. Add the potatoes to the skillet with the onion and cilantro mixture.
Toss everything together to combine and cook for 1-2 minutes, until the potatoes are well coated with the flavorful mixture.
Serve the potato mixture in tacos, garnished with additional cilantro and lime wedges if desired.
